The misdirected et~el'gies fail to
achieve the ains and hit thE! ta(9et; the time and efforts are wasted.
The weak Buddha ( r.1er'cury} shows the absence of the capacity to
plan. scheme and organize. the approach to problems is unpractical;
the ttli nking is unrealistic; the calculations <~re faulty: the judgments are
incorrect: the intelligence is below normal and second-rate and
consequently the execution of the plans is defective. Once in diffiwky
the person concerned falls to find his way out as he Is not bubbling with
ideas, a gift whl<h a good and strong Buddha (Mercury) confers. Such
a person cannot be Independent: he must depend upon others for his
existence:.
A weak Buddha (Mercury) in concurrence with some other factors
sometimes makes one lose one's bonafide: the person concerned
becomes maicious and vicious: his thinking is destructive and his means
of livelil1ood are questionable and sometime even illegal; he is cunning,
crooked and completely undependable.
